attributes.content:
## Overview

The Symptom Log card gives the workspace a structured home for daily symptom tracker with severity, notes, triggers, and timeline visualization.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Symptom Log acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Symptom Log can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Report]]** -- Symptom Log can feed rollups and summaries that surface trends, status, or outcomes.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Symptom Log can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Symptom Log.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Symptom Log can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Contact]]** -- Symptom Log often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Health Record]]** -- Health Record can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Symptom Log.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Symptom Log as the structured home for daily symptom tracker with severity, notes, triggers, and timeline visualization and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Symptom Log when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Symptom Log stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Symptom Log
cardInfo.summary: Daily symptom tracker with severity, notes, triggers, and timeline visualization.
